violations.text: Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Product was relocated. Proper food storage hand out provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of bio-film. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was turned off and ice was disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the inside of the prep units have an accumulation of food residue/debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

The floors and walls near the fryolator and near the 3-bay sink have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

Sanitizer bucket was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Bucket was relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution for the dish machine was observed in excess of 100 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment will switch to 3-bay sink until dish machine is adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pasta was placed hot in the refrigerator in covered containers. Food was relocated and uncovered. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

The following equipment was observed in poor repair: Sanitizer dispenser above 3-bay sink. Dispenser was not in use at time of inspection Fryolator basket was observed with frayed metal. Fryolator basket disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine and quaternary ammonium sanitizing solutions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of debris: -Shelving above hot hold soup and hot hold meatball containers -Non food contact surfaces of ice machine. Ice machine to be turned off -and sanitized. -Walk-In vent cover -A/C unit New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

Meatballs, which was previously cooked and cooled, was only reheated to 114-128 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Food was reheated on a stove top. Proper reheating reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2024

Cups without handles are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2024

Exposed wood is used in the construction of shelving in the 3-bay area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2024
The portable fan and magnetic strips used for storing utensils were observed with an accumulation of dust and debris. Equipment to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2024

The grease trap outside is located on grass. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024
The ceiling in the kitchen area are covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024
